title: Generic Account Prohibition ‚Äî `admin` / `root` Interactive Success on CDE

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 2.2.2 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 2.2.2 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.52: Generic Account Prohibition ‚Äî `admin` / `root` Interactive Success on CDE.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.52' running on index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" or auditd ‚Äî user, tty, src_ip, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
spl:
index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"Accepted password|Accepted publickey")
| rex "for (?<acct>\\S+) from (?<src_ip>\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)"
| where acct IN ("root","admin","Administrator")
| lookup pci_linux_payment_hosts.csv host OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| stats count by host, acct, src_ip
| sort - count
description: Alerts on successful interactive use of generic superuser accounts on in-scope Linux payment hosts, enforcing unique IDs and eliminating shared root for routine administration.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes linux and sourcetypes linux:secure. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Prefer `auditd` TYPE=USER_LOGIN for richer fields if available. (2) Allow break-glass from bastion subnet only via `where`. (3) Immediate SOC page. (4) Quarterly attestation of zero violations goal. Indexes required: `index=linux`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=linux:secure`; Lookups: `pci_linux_payment_hosts`, `pci_linux_payment_hosts.csv`.
visualization: Table (violations), map (src_ip), single value (weekly violation count).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.Intrusion_Detection by Intrusion_Detection.signature, Intrusion_Detection.dest, Intrusion_Detection.severity | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:secure"` or `auditd` ‚Äî `user`, `tty`, `src_ip`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Prefer `auditd` TYPE=USER_LOGIN for richer fields if available. (2) Allow break-glass from bastion subnet only via `where`. (3) Immediate SOC page. (4) Quarterly attestation of zero violations goal.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"Accepted password|Accepted publickey")
| rex "for (?<acct>\\S+) from (?<src_ip>\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)"
| where acct IN ("root","admin","Administrator")
| lookup pci_linux_payment_hosts.csv host OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| stats count by host, acct, src_ip
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Generic Account Prohibition ‚Äî `admin` / `root` Interactive Success on CDE (PCI DSS Req 8.2.6, 2.2.2)** ‚Äî Alerts on successful interactive use of generic superuser accounts on in-scope Linux payment hosts, enforcing unique IDs and eliminating shared root for routine administration.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:secure"` or `auditd` ‚Äî `user`, `tty`, `src_ip`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: linux; **sourcetype**: linux:secure. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=linux, sourcetype="linux:secure", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"Accepted password|Accepted publickey")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where acct IN ("root","admin","Administrator")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where pci_host="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, acct, src_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Generic Account Prohibition ‚Äî `admin` / `root` Interactive Success on CDE (PCI DSS Req 8.2.6, 2.2.2)** ‚Äî Alerts on successful interactive use of generic superuser accounts on in-scope Linux payment hosts, enforcing unique IDs and eliminating shared root for routine administration.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:secure"` or `auditd` ‚Äî `user`, `tty`, `src_ip`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (violations), map (src_ip), single value (weekly violation count).
grandmaExplanation: We alerts on successful interactive use of generic superuser accounts on in-scope Linux payment hosts, enforcing unique IDs and eliminating shared root for routine administration. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Intrusion_Detection (CIM data model).
